skill.name: plans-management
skill.description: Helps Claude Code and Codex maintain a Plans.md task file using consistent sections, status markers, completion dates, and archive rules.
skill.summary: Manage Plans.md tasks, status markers, and task archives in a consistent project workflow.
skill.author: Chachamaru127

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command detections and two high-risk heuristic detections, but review shows the flagged content is Markdown task syntax, status markers, and documentation examples. No malicious code, prompt injection, network access, credential access, or executable payload was found; the remaining risk is expected filesystem editing of Plans.md.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Editing Capability
low_findings.description: The skill is designed to read, write, and edit Plans.md. This is expected for task management, but users should review changes because it can modify project files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The front matter explicitly allows Read, Write, and Edit, and the body describes Plans.md updates. This is legitimate functionality, but it is still a real file modification capability.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The numerous Ruby or shell backtick detections are inline Markdown markers and fenced examples, not executable code. The grep examples are documentation for searching Plans.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are inside Markdown code fences, tables, or inline marker examples. No runtime code path executes these strings.

low_findings.title: Static Obfuscation And Weak Crypto Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-entropy and weak cryptography alerts do not correspond to binary content, encrypted payloads, or cryptographic implementation. The files contain Japanese documentation and Markdown metadata.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found human-readable Markdown at the flagged locations. No obfuscated block, decoder, cipher, hash implementation, or suspicious payload was present.

content.value_statement: Teams lose project context when task status is written in inconsistent formats. This skill keeps Plans.md organized with clear sections, markers, dates, and archive rules.
actual_capabilities: Adds new tasks to the correct Plans.md section with supported status markers.
Moves tasks through requested states such as TODO, WIP, complete, confirmed, and blocked.
Applies consistent marker formatting with backticks and completion dates.
Maintains standard Plans.md sections for active, pending, completed, and archived tasks.
Documents blocked tasks with reasons, owners, and expected resolution dates.
Supports optional task IDs, dependencies, and parallelizable task markers.
limitations: Only covers Plans.md task management and does not implement project work itself.
Requires an existing Plans.md convention or user approval before creating one.
Does not validate task truth against external issue trackers or calendars.
Uses documented marker conventions and may not match every team workflow.

use_cases.title: Keep Engineering Tasks Current
use_cases.description: Update Plans.md when starting work, finishing work, or recording blocked tasks during a coding session.

use_cases.title: Coordinate PM Review
use_cases.description: Track requested, completed, and confirmed tasks with clear markers that separate implementation from review.

use_cases.title: Archive Project History
use_cases.description: Move older completed tasks into archive sections while preserving dates and status for future reference.

prompt_templates.title: Add a Task
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single new task
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a task to Plans.md for updating the login form validation. Include a short detail line and use the standard pending marker.

prompt_templates.title: Mark Work Started
prompt_templates.scenario: Move one task into active work
prompt_templates.prompt: Update Plans.md to show that I started the payment API task. Move it to the active section and use the correct WIP marker.

prompt_templates.title: Complete and Summarize
prompt_templates.scenario: Finish a task with completion metadata
prompt_templates.prompt: Mark the profile settings task complete in Plans.md. Add today's date, keep related file notes, and preserve the section order.

prompt_templates.title: Normalize a Project Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Review Plans.md and normalize all task markers, blocked reasons, dates, dependencies, and archive placement without changing task meaning.

output_examples.input: Add a new task for password reset email tests.
output_examples.output: A pending Plans.md task is added with the standard marker, a concise task title, and a detail line for email test coverage.

output_examples.input: Mark the dashboard analytics task as blocked.
output_examples.output: The task is updated to blocked status with a visible reason, owner, and expected resolution date when provided.

output_examples.input: Archive completed tasks from last month.
output_examples.output: Older confirmed tasks are moved into the archive section under the correct month while their completion dates remain intact.

best_practices: Review the planned Plans.md change before accepting edits in shared repositories.
Keep task names specific enough to identify the work without opening other files.
Record blocked reasons and owners when changing a task to blocked status.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for implementation, code review, or files unrelated to Plans.md.
Do not remove empty standard sections because they preserve the expected workflow.
Do not mix unsupported marker formats with the documented backtick marker style.

faq.question: Does this skill execute commands?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed files are Markdown guidance. The listed grep commands are examples for users who want to search Plans.md.

faq.question: Can it create a Plans.md file?
faq.answer: Yes, when the user asks for one or approves the format. It follows the documented section order and marker rules.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Can it manage tasks outside Plans.md?
faq.answer: No. The skill scope is Plans.md task management and related marker updates.

faq.question: Does it integrate with issue trackers?
faq.answer: No. It does not sync with external systems. It only edits the local planning document.

faq.question: Why is the security risk low instead of safe?
faq.answer: The skill can edit project files, which is expected but still requires normal review before accepting changes.
